es.meaning: To be stunned; frozen in shock.
es.example: Cuando me lo contó, me quedé de piedra.
es.exampleTranslation: When she told me, I was stunned.
es.regionNote: Peninsular-leaning. Mexican speakers often prefer 'quedarse helado'. Reviewer to confirm LatAm currency.

it.meaning: To be left speechless with shock.
it.example: Sono rimasto di sasso alla notizia.
it.exampleTranslation: I was stunned at the news.

bridge.note: Twin idioms — both turn you to stone, both use a 'remain' verb.

interference.es_to_it: HIGH VALUE: rimanere takes essere — sono rimasto di sasso. Spanish speakers reach for 'ho rimasto'. This is a flagship interference entry.

review.notes: Best auxiliary-trap teaching entry in the bank so far. Overlaps deliberately with 0027 (quedarse/rimanere, the bare collocation).

hook.headline: You will say ho rimasto. No Italian ever has.
hook.subhead: Spanish has one perfect auxiliary. Italian has two, and rimanere takes the other one.
